user_message: Quick one -- map the stakeholders on Fabrikam Residences.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Kevin Lee <kevin.lee@fabrikamresi.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-23 13:56 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Fabrikam Residences escalation triage

Just checking in on the escalation triage proposal. Our VP Operations is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Kevin Lee <kevin.lee@fabrikamresi.com>
Date: 2024-02-05 09:08 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Fabrikam Residences escalation triage

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with escalation triage. What are the next steps from your side?

Meeting: escalation triage Discussion - Fabrikam Residences
Date: 2024-03-18
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on escalation triage from our last session.

[00:06:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): The timeline for vendor dependencies is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:09:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): The timeline for timeline constraints is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:11:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

[00:16:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:19:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about partner engagement. Can we address those?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-K trap (One-page constraint hurts complex meetings); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-K failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-K failure mode (One-page constraint hurts complex meetings)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-K -- One-page constraint hurts complex meetings
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
